alerts.title: U.S. launches retaliatory strikes on Iran following helicopter downing; mutual escalation cycle accelerates
alerts.summary: Following Iran's downing of a U.S. Army helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz on June 9, the U.S. conducted retaliatory airstrikes against Iranian targets on June 10. Iran subsequently struck Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan in response, marking the first direct U.S.-Iran kinetic exchange of the conflict theater. Multiple tier-1 outlets (NPR, AP News, Reuters, Bloomberg, WSJ) confirm active strike cycle. Threat level remains at 9 (CRITICAL).
alerts.region: Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf, Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan

alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment confirmed at 60% U-235; nuclear breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: Iran's uranium enrichment level confirmed at 60% U-235 with estimated breakout time of approximately 1-2 weeks, per global_metrics nuclear feed and coordinated IAEA/Western pressure campaign (Reuters, Jerusalem Post, Al Jazeera, CSIS). Concurrent with active U.S.-Iran kinetic engagement and elevated proxy activity, Iran now faces dual escalation pathways: immediate military and near-term nuclear threshold crossing. IAEA and U.S. draft resolutions demand Iranian transparency on nuclear sites.

alerts.title: Tehran-linked cyber unit claims widespread Israeli radar system compromise; escalation to cyber domain underway
alerts.summary: Iranian cyber actors have asserted responsibility for penetrating Israeli radar systems, per cyber_headlines in global_metrics feed (Shafagh News). This represents coordinated escalation across kinetic, nuclear, and cyber domains following June 8-10 missile exchanges. CSIS and Industrial Cyber reports document surge in cyber retaliation targeting government, defense, and critical infrastructure sectors in Middle East theater.

alerts.title: Israel kills 14 in southern Lebanon airstrike; Hezbollah escalation risk elevated amid Iran-Israel cycle
alerts.summary: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed 14 individuals on June 9-10, per Al Jazeera and Guardian reporting, following earlier strike on Tyre that killed eight. Evacuation orders issued for Lebanese Christian quarter. Incidents cluster with broader Iran-Israel missile exchange and directly implicate Hezbollah theater as spillover zone. Risk of Hezbollah retaliation and broader regional conflagration elevated.

alerts.title: U.S. domestic political discourse dominated by Iran crisis; negative sentiment spike amid escalation
alerts.summary: Sentiment analysis shows U.S. news corpus at 47% negative, 45% positive with dominant keywords 'trump,' 'iran,' and 'strikes' (50, 20, 9 mentions respectively). Helicopter incident dominates U.S. discourse alongside domestic political coverage (Maine primary, Senate races). Rapid sentiment polarity shift reflects acute public concern over kinetic escalation and nuclear timeline.

analysis_note: The U.S.-Iran conflict theater has entered acute kinetic and nuclear crisis phase with direct military exchange, 60% uranium enrichment, cyber retaliation, and Hezbollah spillover all active simultaneously; threat level critical (9/10) with breakout window measured in weeks.
